# Oliver Aleksander Larsen — Software Architecture Researcher

> Software architecture researcher at the University of Southern Denmark (SDU)
> studying how architecture adapts when AI becomes both a design tool and a
> design subject. Four papers accepted at IEEE ICSA 2026 (Amsterdam).

## Research Areas
- Software Architecture for AI-Augmented Systems
- Edge Computing and IoT
- AI Governance and EU AI Act Compliance
- Blockchain Architecture (Bitcoin-native)
- LLM-Driven Middleware and Interoperability

## Papers at IEEE ICSA 2026

### Edge-Based Standing-Water Detection via FSM-Guided Tiering and Multi-Model Consensus
- Authors: Oliver Larsen, Mahyar T. Moghaddam
- Venue: IEEE ICSA 2026, Software Architecture in Practice Track
- Keywords: edge computing, FSM orchestration, model tiering, multi-model consensus, sensor fusion



- Code: https://github.com/Oliver1703dk/flood_detection_system

**Abstract:** Standing water detection on agricultural edge devices (Raspberry Pi + Jetson). We present an FSM-guided control plane that selects between local and offloaded inference tiers based on resource availability and environmental conditions. A multi-model YOLO ensemble combined with diurnal sensor fusion achieves robust detection across varying lighting and weather conditions, enabling real-time flood monitoring on resource-constrained hardware.

**TL;DR:** We built an edge AI system that detects standing water on farms using a state-machine to intelligently switch between cheap and expensive ML models depending on device resources.

### RAD-AI: Rethinking Architecture Documentation for AI-Augmented Ecosystems
- Authors: Oliver Aleksander Larsen, Mahyar T. Moghaddam
- Venue: ANGE 2026 Workshop at IEEE ICSA 2026
- Keywords: software architecture, architecture documentation, AI-augmented systems, arc42, C4 model, EU AI Act
- arXiv: https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.28735


- Code: https://github.com/Oliver1703dk/RAD-AI

**Abstract:** We propose a backward-compatible extension of arc42 (adding 8 sections) and C4 (adding 3 diagram types) specifically designed for AI-augmented ecosystems. Our extensions enable systematic documentation of AI components, their data flows, training pipelines, and regulatory requirements. When evaluated against EU AI Act Annex IV requirements, RAD-AI improves documentation addressability from approximately 36% to 93%. We validate the framework on two industrial case studies: Uber Michelangelo and Netflix Metaflow.

**TL;DR:** We extended two popular architecture documentation frameworks (arc42 and C4) to handle AI systems, improving EU AI Act compliance coverage from ~36% to ~93%.

### BitSov: A Composable Bitcoin-Native Architecture for Sovereign Internet Infrastructure
- Authors: Oliver Aleksander Larsen, Rasmus Thorsen Larsen, Mahyar T. Moghaddam
- Venue: BlockArch 2026 Workshop at IEEE ICSA 2026
- Keywords: blockchain architecture, Bitcoin, Lightning Network, sovereign infrastructure, decentralized identity
- arXiv: https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.28727




**Abstract:** We present BitSov, an eight-layer composable architectural framework for Bitcoin-native sovereign internet infrastructure. The framework introduces three architectural patterns: payment-gated messaging, timechain-locked contracts, and a self-sustaining economic flywheel. BitSov employs dual settlement across on-chain Bitcoin transactions and Lightning Network micropayments, enabling censorship-resistant communication and identity management without reliance on traditional internet infrastructure.

**TL;DR:** An eight-layer architecture that uses Bitcoin and Lightning Network to build censorship-resistant internet infrastructure — with payment-gated messaging and decentralized identity.

### SAGAI-MID: A Generative AI-Driven Middleware for Dynamic Runtime Interoperability
- Authors: Oliver Aleksander Larsen, Mahyar T. Moghaddam
- Venue: SAGAI 2026 Workshop at IEEE ICSA 2026
- Keywords: software architecture, interoperability, LLMs, middleware, runtime adaptation
- arXiv: https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.28731


- Code: https://github.com/Oliver1703dk/sagai-mid

**Abstract:** We present SAGAI-MID, a FastAPI-based middleware that uses large language models to dynamically resolve schema mismatches at runtime. The system implements a five-layer pipeline with hybrid detection, dual resolution strategies (DIRECT transformation vs. CODEGEN), and a three-tier safeguard stack. Evaluated across 10 interoperability scenarios using 6 different LLMs, the best configuration achieves 0.90 pass@1 accuracy, demonstrating practical viability of LLM-driven runtime schema adaptation.

**TL;DR:** A middleware that uses LLMs to automatically fix API schema mismatches at runtime — achieving 90% accuracy across 10 test scenarios and 6 different language models.

### Hybrid Large-Language-Model Meal Recommender System for Athletes: A Short Communication
- Authors: Oliver Aleksander Larsen, Vinay Chakravarthi Gogineni
- Venue: Results in Engineering, Under Revision
- Keywords: LLM, LoRA, PEFT, meal recommendation, sports nutrition, Mistral-7B, in-context learning, recommender system

- SSRN: https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=6233133
- ResearchGate: https://www.researchgate.net/publication/400770955


**Abstract:** Athletes often struggle with static, rule-based meal plans that fail to adapt to evolving personal preferences and feedback, leading to reduced adherence and suboptimal nutrition for performance. This short communication introduces a hybrid large-language-model (LLM)-driven recommender system to personalize meal recommendations within CarboPlanner, a sports nutrition platform. The system combines in-context learning (ICL) for short-term adaptation via dynamic prompts incorporating user profiles, recent feedback, and meal candidates, with parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT) using low-rank adaptation (LoRA) on the Mistral-7B-Instruct model for long-term, system-wide learning from accumulated feedback. Implemented as a modular Flask API integrated seamlessly into CarboPlanner's existing infrastructure (Flutter app, NestJS backend, and MongoDB database), the system processes athlete feedback through dual loops: immediate ICL updates and periodic PEFT retraining. Evaluation on 1000 test prompts from a held-out dataset demonstrated superior performance over baselines. The hybrid model achieved 46.7% prediction accuracy and 100% format adherence, outperforming the non-fine-tuned ICL-only model (41.5% accuracy, 39.7% format adherence) and random selection (20% accuracy, 100% format adherence).

**TL;DR:** A hybrid LLM recommender for CarboPlanner that combines in-context learning with LoRA fine-tuned Mistral-7B — achieving 46.7% accuracy and 100% format adherence, outperforming ICL-only and random baselines.
